channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Championship play-off final may be delayed by Spygate hearing
item.description: The Championship play-off final may not go ahead on Saturday 23 May as the EFL investigates the Spygate scandal surrounding finalists Southampton.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v Blossoms & Songer
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on indie band Blossoms and rapper Songer plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for the FA Cup final and this weekend's Premier League fixtures.

item.title: Parlour? Villa? Gerrard? Ranking best FA Cup final goals
item.description: From Ricky Villa to Steven Gerrard, Ray Parlour to Roberto di Matteo - here are 10 of the best FA Cup final goals of all time.

item.title: Man City beat rivals Man Utd 2-1 to win FA Youth Cup
item.description: Reigan Heskey smashes home a late winner as Manchester City beat Manchester United to win the FA Youth Cup.

item.title: Shaw leads WSL player of the season shortlist
item.description: Manchester City striker Khadija Shaw on an eight-strong shortlist for player of the season at the 2025-26 Women's Super League Football Awards.

item.title: Kerr to leave Chelsea at end of season
item.description: Chelsea's all-time top scorer in the WSL, Sam Kerr, will leave the club when her contract expires this summer.

item.title: How controversial VAR calls have defined two title battles
item.description: England and Scotland have experienced two highly controversial VAR decisions in a matter of days which could help to decide who ends the season as champions.

item.title: McCabe to leave Arsenal in the summer
item.description: Republic of Ireland captain Katie McCabe will leave Arsenal when her contract expires at the end of the season.

item.title: Tavernier hurt at losing farewell Ibrox appearance
item.description: Departing Rangers captain James Tavernier says he "felt deeply hurt and let down" and that "the situation could and should have been handled much better" after missing out on a farewell appearance at Ibrox.

item.title: Nancy rues lack of time after 'beautiful' 33-day Celtic spell
item.description: Wilfried Nancy describes his 33-day tenure as Celtic manager as a "beautiful experience" but contends it's a "no brainer" that he didn't get long enough to change the culture and impose his way of doings things at the club.

item.title: Controversial 99th-minute penalty boosts Celtic title hopes
item.description: Watch the penalty award against Motherwell that keeps Celtic's Scottish Premiership title hopes alive going into a final-day showdown with Hearts.

item.title: Man City pile pressure on Arsenal with Palace win
item.description: Manchester City beat Crystal Palace 3-0 at the Etihad Stadium, closing the gap on leaders Arsenal to two points in the Premier League.

item.title: Hearts put three past Falkirk to move within point of title
item.description: Watch the highlights as Hearts beat Falkirk 3-0 to set up a final-day meeting with Celtic in which they only need to avoid defeat to win the Scottish Premiership.

item.title: 'Beyond happy' - Guardiola praises Foden performance
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says he is "beyond happy" with Phil Foden's performance after the midfielder provided two assists in City's 3-0 win against Crystal Palace.

item.title: Celtic score controversial late penalty to set up epic final day
item.description: Watch the highlights as Celtic score a controversial late penalty to win at Motherwell and take the Scottish Premiership title race to a final-day decider against Hearts.

item.title: PSG & Arsenal eye Fernandes - Friday's gossip
item.description: Paris St-Germain keen on West Ham midfielder Mateus Fernandes, Manchester City and PSG have interest in Real Madrid midfielder Federico Valverde, Manchester United monitoring Sporting Lisbon midfielder Morten Hjulmand, plus more.

item.title: Elliott's wasted Villa loan embarrassing - Emery
item.description: Harvey Elliott has played only four Premier League games for Aston Villa this season and has not featured since February.

item.title: Hearts call for 'fair opportunity' in title finale
item.description: Hearts' owners have pleaded that destination of the Scottish Premiership title be "decided by the players on the pitch" rather than by the match officials.

item.title: The documentary that transformed a Welsh club into a global brand
item.description: What can you expect from the latest season of 'Welcome to Wrexham' and how has this documentary series transformed a football club in north Wales into a global phenomenon?

item.title: How local boy O'Reilly took centre stage at Man City
item.description: Nico O'Reilly has fond memories of his last trip to a Wembley Stadium final and the Manchester City player will be aiming to produce a repeat in Saturday's FA Cup showpiece against Chelsea.

item.title: Louie Barry - Barcelona to Wembley via nine loans
item.description: From Barcelona to Stockport and a second Wembley final - the rollercoaster career of "wonderkid" Louie Barry.

item.title: Mbappe and Dembele lead France World Cup squad
item.description: Kylian Mbappe and Ballon d'Or holder Ousmane Dembele will lead a star-studded France attack after Didier Deschamps announced his 26-man squad for this summer's World Cup.

item.title: Ancelotti extends Brazil contract until 2030
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti extends his contract as Brazil manager until the 2030 World Cup.

item.title: Man Utd's Carrick on manager of the season shortlist
item.description: Manchester United caretaker head coach Michael Carrick is included on a six-man shortlist for the Premier League's Manager of the Season award.

item.title: FPL talking point - four players for the final stretch
item.description: With two weeks of the Premier League campaign remaining, selecting players with low ownership could give you an end-of-season boost. Here are four to help give you the edge.

item.title: Why Tsaroulla is 'cherishing' play-offs with Notts County
item.description: Notts County's Nick Tsaroulla talks about how surviving a car crash that very nearly ended his career as a teenager has taught him to "cherish" the game.

item.title: Hurzeler unsure if Japan's Mitoma will make World Cup
item.description: Brighton boss Fabian Hurzeler says he "is not sure yet" if Japan winger Kaoru Mitoma will be fit to play in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Captain Gyokeres for a Burnley bonanza - FPL team of the week
item.description: Arsenal's home game against Burnley is the perfect opportunity to capture a late-season FPL haul - but which attacking asset should you pick?

item.title: Taylor named permanent Exeter boss for second time
item.description: Exeter City appointed Matt Taylor as their new manager after returning to the club on an interim basis.

item.title: 'Iran still waiting on World Cup visas'
item.description: Iranian FA president Medhi Taj says players have not been issued with visas for the World Cup, which starts in less than a month.

item.title: Was it a penalty? Celtic and Hearts face title decider as McInnes slams 'disgusting' call
item.description: Hearts head coach Derek McInnes says his side are "up against everybody" after a "disgusting" 99th-minute penalty winner for Celtic ensured the Scottish Premiership title race goes to a final-day decider on Saturday.

item.title: African fans face World Cup issues despite visa bond U-turn
item.description: The Trump administration waives a $15,000 visa bond for fans from five countries who have World Cup tickets, but African fans still face challenges.

item.title: Braintree's Smith in New Zealand World Cup squad
item.description: Defender Tommy Smith, whose club Braintree Town were relegated from the National League this season, is named in New Zealand's World Cup squad.

item.title: Hall, 18, becomes youngest to score MLS hat-trick
item.description: Forward Julian Hall makes MLS history by becoming the youngest player to score a hat-trick in Red Bull New York's win over Columbus Crew.

item.title: Man City were too good for us - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner says Manchester City were "too good" for his side after losing 3-0 at Etihad Stadium.

item.title: Levy optimistic Tottenham will avoid relegation
item.description: Former Tottenham chairman Daniel Levy is optimistic the club will remain in the Premier League despite battling relegation.

item.title: Slavia Prague punished for pitch invasion
item.description: Slavia Prague are fined after a mass pitch invasion sees their match against rivals Sparta Prague abandoned

item.title: Watch goals as Dunfermline & Thistle draw play-off semi first leg
item.description: Watch the goals as Dunfermline Athletic and Partick Thistle draw the first leg of their Scottish Premiership play-off semi-final.

item.title: Calvert-Lewin penalty earns Leeds draw at Tottenham
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur squander the opportunity to take a significant step towards Premier League safety as they are held to a 1-1 draw at home by Leeds United.

item.title: Watch: Mead marks 50th Arsenal goal with stunning double
item.description: Watch Beth Mead score her 50th and 51st WSL goals for Arsenal with the England forward set to depart the club at the end of the season.

item.title: Tottenham players good enough to stay up - De Zerbi
item.description: Tottenham boss Roberto De Zerbi says his players are good enough to stay up after their draw with Leeds United leaves them two points clear above the relegation zone with two games to play.

item.title: How to follow the FA Cup final on the BBC
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain how you can watch, listen and follow the FA Cup final.

item.title: Madonna, Shakira & BTS to headline World Cup show
item.description: Madonna, Shakira and K-pop boy band BTS will headline the Super Bowl-style half-time show at this summer's World Cup final.

item.title: Walsall name ex-Huddersfield boss Grant head coach
item.description: League Two side Walsall appoint Lee Grant as their new head coach, replacing Darren Byfield who was given the job until the end of the season.

item.title: What is financial gap between Old Firm and rest of Scottish Premiership?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at the financial gap between the Old Firm and the rest of Scottish top-flight football.

item.title: Penalty pandemonium sets up Hearts & Celtic showdown for the ages
item.description: On a night of bewilderment, there was one unarguable truth. Hearts still have this in their own hands. Only Celtic can stop them, writes Tom English.

item.title: Dundee United's Ferry in Republic of Ireland squad
item.description: Dundee United defender Will Ferry is called up to the Republic of Ireland squad for Saturday's friendly against Grenada.

item.title: US drops $15,000 visa deposit for foreign fans with World Cup tickets
item.description: Fifty countries, including five that have qualified for the World Cup, are subject to the bond requirement as part of Trump's crackdown on visa overstays.

item.title: Beyond the Hollywood story - what are Wrexham really building?
item.description: BBC Sport looks at how and why Wrexham co-owners Ryan Reynolds and Rob Mac are building more than just a successful men's team.

item.title: 'I sold my late dad's diamond ring to get to Villa's Europa League final'
item.description: Scott Barnes took the emotional decision, to bag a ticket to Villa's first Euro final since 1982.

item.title: It's West Ham or Spurs - how relegation fight is shaping up
item.description: With two games remaining of the Premier League season, Tottenham and West Ham are the two horses left in the race to avoid relegation.

item.title: Forty years of the EFL play-offs - choose your favourite memories
item.description: BBC Sport looks back at 15 memorable moments from 40 years of the play-offs in the English Football League.

item.title: How Gerrard went from 'best night' to 'head like a box of frogs'
item.description: Steven Gerrard inspired Liverpool's Champions League win in 2005 but just two months later wanted out. What happened?

item.title: How do Chelsea rebuild for next season's WSL?
item.description: Chelsea are in an unusual position with one game to go in the Women's Super League and no silverware on the line.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are Fifa's rules on switching nationalities?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the rules on players switching nations in international football.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Motherwell say no bid for Berthel Askou amid Toulouse talk
item.description: Motherwell insist they have received no approach for manager Jens Berthel Askou amid reports linking the Dane with French Ligue 1 side Toulouse.

item.title: Celtic face European competition for Hibs midfielder Chaiwa - gossip
item.description: Celtic continue to pursue Hibs midfielder, while Paul Dickov believes a return to Rangers would benefit Scotland defender Nathan Patterson.

item.title: Scotland await Conway news before Tuesday squad announcement
item.description: Scotland head coach Steve Clarke will announce his squad for this summer's World Cup on Tuesday, by which time he should know whether Tommy Conway is fit to travel if picked.

item.title: Might a former Celtic hero help Haiti surprise Scotland?
item.description: The Haitians, thanks to some savvy scouts and persuasive agents, are putting together a team which hopes to shock Scotland in their opening World Cup game.

item.title: Captain Gorry to leave West Ham
item.description: Katrina Gorry, who will leave West Ham when her contract expires next month, says captaining the side has been "the greatest honour".

item.title: Why Arsenal have fallen short in WSL and Champions League this season
item.description: Why have Renee Slegers' side not been able to get their hands on any of the season's biggest prizes?

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.
